diagnosis_result.doctor_diagnosis: Ms. ___ is a 54-year-old female with a history of peripheral vascular disease and a prior left femoral to posterior tibial bypass who was admitted for evaluation of worsening bilateral toe ulcers and rest pain. She underwent a diagnostic bilateral lower extremity angiogram which revealed extensive arterial disease, including occlusions of the bilateral superficial femoral and popliteal arteries and the previous bypass graft. The procedure was uncomplicated, and by the first postoperative day, she was clinically stable, afebrile, and ambulating with the assistance of a walker. Although she eloped from the hospital prior to formal discharge teaching, she was alert and cooperative with nursing for the removal of her intravenous line. She has been instructed to continue her home medications, excluding lisinopril and rivaroxaban which are held until her next procedure, and to maintain her existing podiatry wound care regimen with Adaptic and Kerlix. She is scheduled to return to the hospital on ___ for a planned redo left femoral to posterior tibial bypass.
diagnosis_result.correct_diagnosis:
The patient is a 54-year-old female who was admitted to the hospital for evaluation of her peripheral vascular disease and toe ulcers. She has a history of left femoral to posterior tibial bypass graft completed in the past, and has been experiencing toe pain and difficulty with ambulation. The patient has a history of ovarian cancer, hypertension, and other medical conditions. She is allergic to penicillins and NSAIDs.

The patient underwent a diagnostic angiogram of her bilateral lower extremities, which showed occlusion of the left SFA, bypass graft, and popliteal artery, as well as occlusion of the right SFA and popliteal artery. She was admitted to the hospital for monitoring and was discharged home without services.

The patient's medications at admission included acetaminophen, lisinopril, gabapentin, mirtazapine, rivaroxaban, atorvastatin, oxyCODONE, and omeprazole. Her medications at discharge included acetaminophen, aspirin, atorvastatin, gabapentin, mirtazapine, omeprazole, and oxyCODONE, with lisinopril and rivaroxaban held until her planned readmission.

The patient's discharge diagnosis was peripheral vascular disease and toe ulcers, and her discharge condition was clear and coherent, with a level of consciousness of alert and interactive, and activity status of ambulatory with assistance or aid.
